problem: atcr debt resolve output changed from "Marked %s resolved." to "Marked %s %s." breaking scripts that parse the output string
fix: Restore the original output string or document the change and update consumers
evidence: [brad] recs, err := localdebt.ReadAll(dir, localdebt.ReadOpts{Writer: cmd.ErrOrStderr()}) scales linearly with history size and risks OOM on long-lived repos / [vera] fmt.Fprintf(..., "Marked %s %s.\n", id, status)
justification:
HIGH|cmd/atcr/debt_resolve.go:250|No length validation on --reason allows multi-MB justification strings to exhaust disk and trigger OOM on subsequent ReadAll calls|Enforce a max length or truncate before assignment|exhaustion|10|rec.Justification = r accepts unbounded input directly from CLI flag
MEDIUM|cmd/atcr/debt_resolve.go:238|Hardcoded "already resolved" message prints even when the record's actual terminal status is wontfix|Substitute the status variable into the success string|correctness|5|fmt.Fprintf(..., "%s is already resolved; nothing to do.\n", id) ignores the passed status parameter
MEDIUM|cmd/atcr/debt_resolve.go:216|markDebtResolved loads the entire append-only store into memory to locate a single finding ID|Replace ReadAll with a targeted seek or fail-fast ID scan|performance|30|recs, err := localdebt.ReadAll(dir, localdebt.ReadOpts{Writer: cmd.ErrOrStderr()}) scales linearly with history size and risks OOM on long-lived repos

problem: (runDebtResolve) The `cmd.Flags().Changed("status")` guard correctly rejects `--status` supplied without `--resolve`, but the error message says "--status/--reason require --resolve <id>" which conflates the two flags. If only `--status` was provided (no `--reason`), the error still mentions `--reason`, which is confusing. The error should mention only the flags that were actually supplied, not all gated flags.
fix: Emit a targeted error listing only the flags that were provided without `--resolve`, e.g. "flag(s) require --resolve <id>" with a list of the offending flags
evidence: [dax] TestDebtResolve_StatusOrReasonWithoutResolveIsUsageError only tests `--status wontfix`; the guard condition `cmd.Flags().Changed("status")` is value-agnostic. / [mira] return usageError(fmt.Errorf("--status/--reason require --resolve <id>"))
justification:
HIGH|cmd/atcr/reconcile_test.go:519|TestPersistLocalDebt_WontfixSuppressesReappend only asserts the wontfix finding is suppressed; it does not add a second, different finding to verify that suppression does not block appending of other new findings. A regression in dedup could silently drop all findings when a wontfix record exists, and this test would still pass.|Extend the test to include an additional finding with a different ID and assert it is appended alongside the suppressed wontfix record.|testing|15|The test body only creates one seed wontfix record and one matching finding; it asserts len(recs)==1 and never introduces a second finding.
MEDIUM|cmd/atcr/debt_resolve.go:240|The already-closed message hardcodes "already resolved" regardless of the existing terminal status (e.g., wontfix). The new wontfix status path is untested for idempotent marks; the pre-existing TestDebtResolve_MarkResolvedIsIdempotent only covers resolved→resolved.|Add a test that marks a wontfix item as res…
